messages.content:
If you mean your device’s RAM usage, here’s the quickest way depending on what you’re using:
Windows


Press Ctrl + Shift + Esc


Open Task Manager


Go to Performance → Memory


You’ll see:


Total RAM


Used RAM


Available RAM


Speed




Mac


Press Cmd + Space → type Activity Monitor


Open it → go to Memory


Check:


Memory Used


Cached Files


Swap Used


Memory Pressure (green = good)




Android


Go to Settings


Search Memory or RAM


On some phones:


Developer Options → Memory




Some brands show RAM in Device Care / Battery & Device Care


iPhone
iPhones usually don’t show RAM directly in Settings, but you can:


Use apps like device info tools


Or tell me your iPhone model, and I can tell you how much RAM it has


Linux
Open Terminal and run:
Bashfree -h
Or for detailed info:
Bashcat /proc/meminfo
If you want me to check your coding environment / program RAM issue
Send me one of these:


a screenshot


your system specs


or output of:


Bashfree -h
If you want, I can also show you how to check RAM from Python / C / Java / Node.js.

`ls`
